content: By 2025, ICM curiosity mechanisms for autonomous agents can be reframed as a recursive self-improvement loop where the agent itself acts as both the explorer of new environments and the curator of its own intrinsic reward signal: each exploration step driven by ICM prediction error expands the agent’s working memory, which in turn rewrites the baseline against which future curiosity is measured. This creates a self-sustaining exploration dynamic that avoids the common 'curiosity collapse' problem of fixed-baseline intrinsic reward methods.
